proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of average treatment effects when the treatment is a high-dimensional textual or visual object summarized by a learned representation that is subject to bounded sup-norm representation distortion against an oracle ground-truth representation, characterizing the sharp identified set of ATEs as a function of representation-distortion budget and the observed-representation outcome regression, recovering point identification under exact representation recovery at the trivial bound and providing a flagship-level non-parametric impossibility result tying any single-representation regression endpoint to a named structural assumption
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains pid_network_exposure, whose headline is sharp ADE bounds from shared local-network completions under misspecified exposure maps. The gap is that pid_network_exposure is graph-completion compatibility, while this proposal is a PartialID learned-representation distortion problem with selector-indexed oracle target fibers, GenAI endpoint calibration, and finite-sample envelope audit bands. ThmSmith doc/API.md Section 3 has no existing representation-distortion or text/image treatment endpoint audit proposal.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012 and ManskiTamer2002 for generic random-set/interval partial identification, ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments for GenAI representation endpoints, and ImbensManski2004/Stoye2009 for inference on partially identified parameters. The epsilon-gap is now workflow-specific: Conjecture 2 does not merely restate interval midpoint algebra; it proposes a nondegenerate two-positive-mass-fiber open failure class for the GenAI plug-in endpoint and a finite-sample robust-sign reporting rule for the learned-representation distortion frontier."}
seed_list: Prior work ImaiNakamura2025GPI would imply that GenAI-extracted representations can support a single causal endpoint for unstructured treatments. Our seed conjectures that the endpoint expands to a sharp distortion interval because an oracle representation within an epsilon sup-norm ball can select different observed-regression values without changing the observed law.

Prior work ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments would imply that a true internal LLM representation restores nonparametric point identification for text treatment effects. Our seed conjectures that approximate internal recovery has a discontinuous phase transition: any positive distortion creates a nondegenerate ATE set unless the observed regression is fiber-invariant.

Prior work VeitchSridharBlei2020 would imply that causally sufficient embeddings preserve all adjustment information needed for causal estimation. Our seed conjectures that epsilon-close embeddings are not approximately sufficient, because two admissible oracle calibrations can agree on the learned embedding law while reversing the oracle ATE sign.

Prior work EgamiFongGrimmerRobertsStewart2022 would predict that split-sample discovered measures control the key identification risk in text causal inference. Our seed conjectures that split-sampling does not shrink the nonparametric distortion identified set, because representation approximation error is an observational equivalence problem rather than overfitting.

Prior work FongGrimmer2023 establishes that randomized texts do not identify latent-treatment effects when unmeasured latent features move together. Our seed conjectures a positive result: bounded sup-norm representation distortion yields the sharp ATE interval exactly, turning their qualitative insufficiency into a quantitative sensitivity frontier.

Prior work KaddourZhuLiuKusnerSilva2021 would imply that structured-treatment embeddings plus generalized Robinson residualization isolate causal effects. Our seed conjectures that residualization cannot rescue a single endpoint under oracle-representation distortion unless a fiber-invariance structural condition holds.

Prior work CorcollAndreuVlontzosORiordanGilliganLee2026 would imply that contrastive structured-treatment representations identify causally relevant factors and discard nuisance factors. Our seed conjectures that contrastive separation still leaves a sharp worst-case ATE diameter whenever the observed regression varies inside an epsilon distortion fiber.

Prior work RobertsStewartNielsen2020 would predict that conditioning on low-dimensional text summaries can address confounding for text-based causal estimates. Our seed conjectures that even perfect adjustment on the learned summary leaves partial identification for oracle text treatments when the summary is only an epsilon-distorted representation of the causal object.

Prior work Ura2018 establishes sharp finite bounds for mismeasured binary endogenous treatment under IV restrictions. Our seed conjectures that high-dimensional representation distortion has a different geometry: no Wald-like scalar endpoint is sharp, but ballwise observed-regression extrema are sharp under distortion-only restrictions.

Prior work BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012 would imply that random-set tools can organize partial ID with missing or set-valued latent variables. Our seed conjectures that learned-representation distortion gives a publishable random-set application where the support function reduces to observed-regression extrema over sup-norm balls.

Prior work ThoratKollaPedanekar2024 would imply that image-treatment neural representations can improve individual effect estimation in zero-shot image settings. Our seed conjectures that zero-shot image effect endpoints require reporting a distortion envelope because unseen images can share learned-neighborhood support but differ in oracle representation.

Prior work HaradaKashima2021 would imply that graph-treatment representations can generalize to unobserved treatments. Our seed conjectures that representation generalization and causal identification separate: an unseen treatment can be representationally close yet causally interval-identified if the outcome regression varies across its distortion ball.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ImaiNakamura2025GPI would imply that GenAI-extracted representations can support a single causal endpoint for unstructured treatments. Our seed conjectures that the endpoint expands to a sharp distortion interval because an oracle representation within an epsilon sup-norm ball can select different observed-regression values without changing the observed law.

anchor_paper.author: Imai and Nakamura
anchor_paper.claim: GPI uses GenAI representations of unstructured text/images to enable causal and predictive inference with uncertainty quantification.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — not an apply-method extension; the surprise is nonpoint identification under certified representation distortion, with a sign-change interval possible from the same observed regression.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski1990 because bounded outcomes always give an interval.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Manski1990 gives generic response bounds, while the epsilon-gap here is a representation-specific sharp envelope indexed by learned-regression extrema over sup-norm distortion balls and tied to GenAI representation workflows.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments would imply that a true internal LLM representation restores nonparametric point identification for text treatment effects. Our seed conjectures that approximate internal recovery has a discontinuous phase transition: any positive distortion creates a nondegenerate ATE set unless the observed regression is fiber-invariant.

anchor_paper.author: Imai and Nakamura
anchor_paper.claim: The paper uses LLM internal representations to disentangle treatment features from confounding features and identifies ATEs for texts as treatments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: the epsilon=0 to epsilon>0 shift exposes loss of point identification rather than extending an estimator to a new setting.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from measurement-error folklore because any measurement error destroys point identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the proposed claim gives the sharp epsilon frontier and exact fiber-invariance exception for learned text representations, not a generic warning.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work VeitchSridharBlei2020 would imply that causally sufficient embeddings preserve all adjustment information needed for causal estimation. Our seed conjectures that epsilon-close embeddings are not approximately sufficient, because two admissible oracle calibrations can agree on the learned embedding law while reversing the oracle ATE sign.

anchor_paper.author: Veitch, Sridhar, and Blei
anchor_paper.claim: The paper develops causally sufficient embeddings that preserve information needed for causal identification from text.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/impossibility, not blocked: it targets the named causally sufficient embedding premise and promotes the obstruction to an open distortion class, not a degenerate witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from EgamiFongGrimmerRobertsStewart2022 because discovered measures can create identification problems.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Egami et al. warn and propose workflow safeguards; they do not characterize the sharp epsilon identified set or the sign-reversal geometry.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work EgamiFongGrimmerRobertsStewart2022 would predict that split-sample discovered measures control the key identification risk in text causal inference. Our seed conjectures that split-sampling does not shrink the nonparametric distortion identified set, because representation approximation error is an observational equivalence problem rather than overfitting.

anchor_paper.author: Egami, Fong, Grimmer, Roberts, and Stewart
anchor_paper.claim: The paper argues that learned text measures can introduce identification problems or overfitting and proposes a split-sample workflow.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — orthogonal to blocklist; the phenomenon separates statistical overfitting from identification under representation distortion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from their paper because they already say learned text measures can introduce identification problems.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: their warning is qualitative; the seed gives a sharp epsilon-indexed identified set and a named condition under which split-sample endpoints are still causal.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FongGrimmer2023 establishes that randomized texts do not identify latent-treatment effects when unmeasured latent features move together. Our seed conjectures a positive result: bounded sup-norm representation distortion yields the sharp ATE interval exactly, turning their qualitative insufficiency into a quantitative sensitivity frontier.

anchor_paper.author: Fong and Grimmer
anchor_paper.claim: Randomization of high-dimensional texts alone is insufficient to identify causal effects of measured latent treatments when unmeasured latent text features confound them.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition, not blocked: the seed turns a named insufficiency mechanism into a sharp sensitivity frontier rather than weakening assumptions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from latent-treatment omitted-variable bias because intervals follow from unmeasured features.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: omitted-variable bias does not yield the exact distortion-ball functional of the observed representation regression.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work KaddourZhuLiuKusnerSilva2021 would imply that structured-treatment embeddings plus generalized Robinson residualization isolate causal effects. Our seed conjectures that residualization cannot rescue a single endpoint under oracle-representation distortion unless a fiber-invariance structural condition holds.

anchor_paper.author: Kaddour, Zhu, Liu, Kusner, and Silva
anchor_paper.claim: The paper proposes generalized Robinson decomposition and Structured Intervention Networks for CATE estimation with structured treatments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/impossibility, not blocked: it targets a named structured-treatment estimator workflow and states an open-class obstruction from representation distortion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just saying their assumptions fail under measurement error.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the proposed impossibility names the exact additional structural condition, fiber-invariance, under which a single endpoint becomes valid.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CorcollAndreuVlontzosORiordanGilliganLee2026 would imply that contrastive structured-treatment representations identify causally relevant factors and discard nuisance factors. Our seed conjectures that contrastive separation still leaves a sharp worst-case ATE diameter whenever the observed regression varies inside an epsilon distortion fiber.

anchor_paper.author: Corcoll Andreu, Vlontzos, O'Riordan, and Gilligan-Lee
anchor_paper.claim: The paper proposes contrastive representations of high-dimensional structured treatments and claims unbiased causal effect estimates under representation assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift, not blocked: the epsilon-fiber variation creates a new diameter term absent from the point-representation claim.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from their own caveat that representation assumptions are needed.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the caveat does not identify the sharp diameter or prove the single-endpoint impossibility frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RobertsStewartNielsen2020 would predict that conditioning on low-dimensional text summaries can address confounding for text-based causal estimates. Our seed conjectures that even perfect adjustment on the learned summary leaves partial identification for oracle text treatments when the summary is only an epsilon-distorted representation of the causal object.

anchor_paper.author: Roberts, Stewart, and Nielsen
anchor_paper.claim: The paper estimates low-dimensional text summaries and conditions on them through matching to address confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — the phenomenon is residual oracle-treatment ambiguity after successful adjustment, not a generic extension of matching.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from text matching not being exact matching.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the seed is not about match imbalance; it assumes the learned-regression object is valid and isolates oracle-representation distortion.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Ura2018 establishes sharp finite bounds for mismeasured binary endogenous treatment under IV restrictions. Our seed conjectures that high-dimensional representation distortion has a different geometry: no Wald-like scalar endpoint is sharp, but ballwise observed-regression extrema are sharp under distortion-only restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Ura
anchor_paper.claim: The paper characterizes sharp identified sets for LATE with mismeasured binary endogenous treatment under IV and monotonicity restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — orthogonal to blocklist; it contrasts binary misclassification geometry with metric learned-representation distortion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Ura2018 by replacing binary cells with representation bins.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: Ura's IV/monotonicity response-type geometry does not yield the distortion-ball regression envelope or the endpoint-impossibility condition.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012 would imply that random-set tools can organize partial ID with missing or set-valued latent variables. Our seed conjectures that learned-representation distortion gives a publishable random-set application where the support function reduces to observed-regression extrema over sup-norm balls.

anchor_paper.author: Beresteanu, Molchanov, and Molinari
anchor_paper.claim: The paper shows how random-set theory simplifies and extends partial-identification analysis.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends method M, potentially blocked; not blocked because the representation-distortion application yields a named single-endpoint impossibility and exact-recovery phase boundary absent from the general random-set paper.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from random-set support functions because balls are random sets.
seed_details.rebuttal: Partly true for the support-function machinery, so this seed is weaker unless paired with the text/image endpoint-impossibility frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ThoratKollaPedanekar2024 would imply that image-treatment neural representations can improve individual effect estimation in zero-shot image settings. Our seed conjectures that zero-shot image effect endpoints require reporting a distortion envelope because unseen images can share learned-neighborhood support but differ in oracle representation.

anchor_paper.author: Thorat, Kolla, and Pedanekar
anchor_paper.claim: The paper proposes NICE for estimating causal effects when treatments are images, including zero-shot settings.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment/target variation, not blocked: the phenomenon is zero-shot oracle ambiguity creating nonpoint ID, not just applying the estimator to images.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an estimator paper, so a partial-ID theorem about representation distortion is a different problem and may not engage the main claim.
seed_details.rebuttal: The engagement is real but secondary; this is a supporting image-treatment seed rather than the chosen flagship angle.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work HaradaKashima2021 would imply that graph-treatment representations can generalize to unobserved treatments. Our seed conjectures that representation generalization and causal identification separate: an unseen treatment can be representationally close yet causally interval-identified if the outcome regression varies across its distortion ball.

anchor_paper.author: Harada and Kashima
anchor_paper.claim: GraphITE learns graph-treatment representations to estimate individual effects and handle zero-shot graph treatments.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends setting from graphs to text/images, potentially blocked; not chosen as flagship because the mechanism is representation-distortion failure, but this anchor is less direct.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from lack of overlap for unseen treatments.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only partly false: overlap and representation distortion are distinct, but the seed is less sharp than the GenAI/text-treatment angle.

proposed_from.literature_map: Manski1990 (AER, Manski1990) gives bounded-outcome treatment-response partial identification; tension: learned text/image representation papers often report point estimates without a weak-assumption identified set. BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012 (Journal of Econometrics, doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2011.06.003) uses random sets/Aumann expectations for partial ID; open use-case: representation-distortion balls as causal random sets. Ura2018 (Quantitative Economics, doi:10.3982/QE886) gives sharp bounds with mismeasured binary endogenous treatment; gap: high-dimensional metric representation error rather than binary misclassification. VeitchSridharBlei2020 (UAI/PMLR, https://proceedings.mlr.press/v124/veitch20a.html) assumes causally sufficient text embeddings; tension: approximate embeddings are not automatically sufficient. RobertsStewartNielsen2020 (AJPS, doi:10.1111/ajps.12526) adjusts for confounding with text summaries; tension: matching quality is not oracle-representation recovery. EgamiFongGrimmerRobertsStewart2022 (Science Advances, doi:10.1126/sciadv.abg2652) warns that discovered text representations create identification risks; open problem: quantify representation error as an ID frontier. FongGrimmer2023 (AJPS, doi:10.1111/ajps.12649) shows randomizing texts alone is insufficient for latent-treatment effects; phenomenon: latent treatment measurement error survives randomization. KaddourZhuLiuKusnerSilva2021 (NeurIPS) estimates CATEs for structured treatments through generalized Robinson decomposition; gap: no sharp nonparametric bounds under representation distortion. ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments (arXiv:2410.00903) uses GenAI internal representations for texts-as-treatments point identification; tension: internal representation must be correct. ImaiNakamura2025GPI (arXiv:2507.03897) extends GenAI-powered inference to unstructured text/images; open diagnostic: how much representation distortion flips conclusions. CorcollAndreuVlontzosORiordanGilliganLee2026 (npj AI, https://www.nature.com/articles/s44387-026-00105-2) claims unbiased causal estimates from contrastive structured-treatment representations; gap: no impossibility boundary for imperfect representations. ThoratKollaPedanekar2024 (arXiv:2412.06810) estimates effects of image treatments; tension: image representation quality is evaluated empirically, not via sharp causal ID.

banked_reason: Stage -0.5 NO-PASS at novelty_target=flagship after 3 angles × 5 revises each (15 attempts). All angles consistently achieved tier=field but flagship floor blocked ACCEPT. Math sound at field tier; the kernel could be re-attempted at --novelty field if desired.
